events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes a field-service visit where ball joint play is observed via a pry test video. This is a suspension/steering component issue. Ball joint play indicates wear/degradation of the ball joint. The pry test showing play means the ball joint is worn/degraded and likely needs replacement.

Let me construct the JSON response.

- Decision: fail (the ball joint has play, which is a defect)
- Risk: high (suspension/steering safety critical)
- Work stage: in-service
- Component conditions: degraded (worn ball joint with play)
- Percent complete: depends on context - this is a diagnostic visit, so maybe 50% (diagnosis done, repair not done)
- Value: 0 (no specific numeric reading given)
- Sound source: not applicable
- Confidence: high - pry test showing play is definitive
- Findings: ball joint play detected during pry test
- Actions: replace ball joint
- Rationale: pry test showing play indicates worn ball joint
- References: OEM service info, alignment specs

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "in-service",
  "component_conditions": ["worn", "degraded"],
  "percent_complete": 30,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.9,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": ["pry-test-ball-joint"],
  "workable": ["replace-ball-joint", "recheck-alignment"],
  "findings": [
    "Ball joint play confirmed by pry test — joint is worn beyond service limit",
    "Steering/suspension safety-critical component degraded while vehicle in service"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace affected ball joint(s) per OEM procedure",
    "Re-check front-end alignment to OEM/alignment specs after replacement",
    "Road test to confirm steering and suspension integrity"
  ],
  "rationale": "A pry test demonstrating visible ball joint play indicates the joint's internal clearance has exceeded the OEM service limit. This is a wear/degradation failure mode of a safety-critical suspension/steering component; the vehicle should not remain in service without correction.",
  "references": [
    "OEM service information — ball joint wear limits and replacement procedure",
    "Alignment specs — post-repair alignment verification"
  ]
}
